items.headline: Comedian Shane Gillis Philadelphia Flyers Eastern 1st Round
items.description:
Comedian Shane Gillis got things going at Xfinity Mobile Arena on Wednesday, before the Philadelphia Flyers and Pittsburgh Penguins dropped the puck in Game 3 of the Eastern Conference First Round.
Gillis – a Flyers superfan – was joined by team mascot Gritty to “ignite the orange” before the game.

items.headline: Buffalo Sabres look to pick up pace in Game 3
items.description:
BUFFALO -- The Buffalo Sabres know what their identity is.
They play with speed and pace from the first line to the fourth line and the top defense pair to the third pair. They can strike quickly, especially with an active and mobile back end, not allowing the opposition time

items.headline: Senators Sanderson ready for increased workload in Game 3 against Hurricanes
items.description:
KANATA, Ontario -- Everywhere you looked in Game 2 of the Eastern Conference First Round, there was defenseman Jake Sanderson.
He played a game-high 43:06 for the Ottawa Senators in a 3-2 double-overtime loss to the Carolina Hurricanes at Lenovo Center.
It's an astounding number, but it is on par

items.headline: Canadiens ready for more playoff hockey in Montreal
items.description:
MONTREAL -- Hockey, they say in these parts, is a religion.
That claim isn’t just Montreal Canadiens fans talking the talk. To their credit, they walk the walk.
Right into their own personal house of worship.

items.headline: Colorado Avalanche Martin Necas continuing unreal season in playoffs
items.description:
DENVER -- Martin Necas held the puck for one extra moment or two. The Colorado Avalanche forward then flicked a pass between the legs of Los Angeles Kings goaltender Anton Forsberg to a wide-open Gabriel Landeskog, who scored the game-tying goal with 3:35 remaining in the third period.

items.headline: Baggie Day Buzz latest news from eliminated NHL teams April 20, 2026
items.description:
For 16 NHL teams, there are no Stanley Cup Playoffs. Instead, players are packing up their gear, reflecting on the season and looking toward the future, as well as front office members analyzing what went wrong.

items.headline: Carolina Hurricanes 'have to do better' at face-offs in Game 3 vs Ottawa
items.description:
RALEIGH, N.C. -- Though the Carolina Hurricanes are off to the 2-0 lead they wanted in the Eastern Conference First Round against the Ottawa Senators, their success in the face-off circle will shape the outcome of the best-of-7 series.
That was the message coach Rod Brind'Amour delivered after practice Wednesday

items.headline: Kings Artemi Panarin looks to reignite offense in Game 3 against Avalanche
items.description:
DENVER -- Artemi Panarin talked as he was walking to the Los Angeles Kings’ team bus, the forward mentioning the tension he felt being back in the Stanley Cup Playoffs.
“Nervous, (but) it’s not about pressure,” said Panarin, playing in the postseason for the first time since 2023-24, while with

items.headline: Mitch Marner's versatility big advantage for Vegas in 2026 playoffs
items.description:
LAS VEGAS -- What is Mitch Marner's role on the Vegas Golden Knights?
First-line left wing? Second-line center? Power-play quarterback? Penalty killer?
The answer is yes.
The 28-year-old has handled a lot from game to game and within games all season, while adjusting to a new team after playing his

items.headline: Minnesota Wild Mats Zuccarello Yakov Trenin Game 3 injury updates
items.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. -- Mats Zuccarello and Yakov Trenin will each be a game-time decision for the Minnesota Wild in Game 3 of the Western Conference First Round against the Dallas Stars at Grand Casino Arena on Wednesday (9:30 p.m. ET; HBO MAX, FDSNWI, FDSNNO, Victory+, truTV, TNT, SN360, TVAS).

items.headline: Boston Bruins second line responds in game 2 win against Buffalo Sabres
items.description:
BUFFALO -- Marco Sturm knew what he was doing.
The Boston Bruins coach did not beat around the bush after Game 1 of the Eastern Conference First Round against the Buffalo Sabres when he said he needed more from his second line, calling them “just OK” when pushed.
As he
